# paren-fuzz seed cases — known-mismatch regressions pinned for replay.
# Lines beginning with `#` and blank lines are ignored. Each SQL is
# replayed as an additional probe after the PRNG-generated batch.
#
# Category A: trailing SELECT after paren-wrapped subquery.
#   PG rejects with 42601 syntax_error at the second SELECT; omni
#   Parse() currently accepts by taking only the first statement. Real
#   bug; fix belongs in omni's top-level statement-list parser, not the
#   paren-dispatch code — tracked outside Phase 1 of pg-paren-dispatch.
SELECT * FROM (SELECT 1) SELECT 1
SELECT * FROM (VALUES (1), (2)) SELECT 1
SELECT * FROM (WITH w AS (SELECT 1) SELECT * FROM w) SELECT 1

# Category B: inner JOIN missing ON/USING inside parens.
#   PG rejects with 42601 ("syntax error at or near `)`"); omni accepts
#   as JoinExpr with nil qual. Already documented in
#   paren_oracle_from_degenerate_test.go (§2.7 "inner JOIN missing qual")
#   — the fuzz harness rediscovers it at depth, confirming the bug
#   surfaces on more than the hand-written canonical case.
SELECT * FROM (T JOIN U)
SELECT * FROM (foo LEFT JOIN W)
SELECT * FROM (foo RIGHT OUTER JOIN W)
